text.title: Hierarchical Pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that recur in hierarchical manager-worker runs, with detector + fix.

antipattern.description: Plan has a cycle (a1 depends on a2 which depends on a1) and the orchestrator dispatches anyway — run hangs at gather time.
antipattern.reason: Detect: plan-time DAG check; verifier flags any back-edge in `deps[]`. Fix: refuse the plan; feed cycle nodes back to manager for re-planning with explicit cycle-detection instructions.

antipattern.description: Manager emits free-form prose instead of JSON; orchestrator silently skips workers it can't parse.
antipattern.reason: Detect: manager output not parseable as JSON. Fix: one structured repair attempt — feed parse error back to manager with the JSON Schema; if still failing, fall back to `abort` policy.

antipattern.description: Manager system prompt blends coordination + domain work ("You are a project manager AND senior developer").
antipattern.reason: Detect: validator finds verbs like "implement"/"write code"/"research" in manager.system_prompt. Fix: strip domain verbs; restate as "Decompose tasks and coordinate workers" only.

antipattern.description: Workers communicate with each other directly (peer messaging) — debug traces become combinatorial and replay impossible.
antipattern.reason: Detect: trace shows worker→worker message edges. Fix: every message goes through the orchestrator; if workers need each other's output, that becomes an explicit `deps[]` edge in the next plan revision.

antipattern.description: Partial failure swept under "best-effort" semantics — one worker times out and the synthesis silently proceeds without its contribution.
antipattern.reason: Detect: trace shows `status==timeout` AND synthesis still emitted without flagging. Fix: declare `failure_policy` (abort/degrade/retry); on `degrade`, surface the missing worker in the synthesis output (`partial=true`).
